_note: Workspace capability descriptions used by the LLM intent router (P5-FUT-006). Edit to tune routing accuracy. Version bumped when workspaces added/changed. Bumped to 2.0 by alias-retirement Phase 7 (DESIGN_ROUTER_CANONICALIZATION_V1.md): the 10 pre-collapse alias entries (auto-agentic, auto-blueteam, auto-redteam, auto-redteam-deep, auto-pentest, auto-purpleteam, auto-purpleteam-deep, auto-purpleteam-exec, auto-mistral, auto-phi4) are retired — their intent is folded into the base descriptions below, and a post-classification keyword pass (_infer_variant in routing.py, using _CODING_VARIANT_SIGNALS/_SECURITY_VARIANT_SIGNALS) recovers the specific variant the LLM used to select directly. The LLM now only ever classifies into a canonical base workspace id.
auto: General conversation, questions that do not clearly fit any specialist workspace, greetings, casual chat.
auto-audio: Audio analysis, podcast summarization, speech transcription, voice notes. Does NOT generate audio — use auto-music or auto-video for generation.
auto-bigfix: IBM BigFix endpoint management, HCL BigFix REST API queries, Bigfix relevance language, fixlet authoring, patch compliance, endpoint remediation scripts, Tivoli.
auto-cad: 3D modeling, OpenSCAD parametric design, STL/OBJ file generation, FDM 3D printing design, CAD code generation, mechanical parts, enclosures, brackets, spur gears.
auto-coding: Code generation and debugging in any language, code review, refactoring, unit tests, APIs, algorithms, data structures, Dockerfiles, CI/CD, PowerShell, Python, Bash, Terraform, Ansible, multi-file edits, large codebase analysis, full-project refactors.
auto-compliance: NERC CIP compliance, regulatory gap analysis, policy mapping to standards, CIP requirement citations, remediation steps, audit preparation, control evidence collection.
auto-creative: Creative writing, fiction, storytelling, poetry, worldbuilding, content generation, marketing copy, narrative.
auto-daily: Everyday personal assistant tasks, quick factual questions, scheduling, email drafting, summaries, general productivity. Also covers STEM reasoning, chain-of-thought, mathematics, science, and rigorous multi-step technical analysis requests.
auto-data: Data analysis, statistics, visualization guidance, exploratory data analysis, data cleaning strategies, metric interpretation, business intelligence.
auto-documents: Creating Word documents, Excel spreadsheets, PowerPoint presentations, and diarized audio transcripts (with speaker labels) using MCP tool servers.
auto-math: Mathematics, equations, symbolic computation, calculus, linear algebra, probability, statistics, numerical methods, step-by-step solutions.
auto-music: Generating music and audio tracks via AudioCraft/MusicGen, describing musical ideas, composition, beat generation.
auto-reasoning: Complex multi-step analysis, research synthesis, pros/cons evaluation, trade-off comparison, detailed breakdowns, deep dives, step-by-step explanations of complex topics. Also covers structured strategic reasoning, business analysis, and planning with explicit thinking-mode requests (e.g. Magistral/Mistral-lineage model requests) — these are the same discipline, strategic analysis under extended reasoning.
auto-research: Web research, information synthesis, fact-checking, literature review, summarising external sources, current events research.
auto-security: Defensive and offensive security analysis, vulnerability assessment, threat hunting, incident response, malware analysis, MITRE ATT&CK, YARA/Sigma rules, CVEs, security hardening. Covers the full spectrum from pure-generation red-team simulation (exploit methodology, C2 frameworks, privilege escalation, lateral movement, penetration testing techniques) through blue-team defense (SOC operations, SIEM analysis, EDR/XDR response, log analysis, malware containment) to integrated purple-team analysis (attack chains combined with matching detection rules, IR playbooks, Sigma/YARA artifacts) and authorized live-execution engagements (real bash/Python commands against authorized targets, AD attack chains, hash cracking, PoC validation, detection engineering) — one discipline, many roles within it.
auto-spl: Splunk SPL queries, search processing language, tstats, inputlookup, outputlookup, correlation searches, notable events, data model acceleration, Splunk Enterprise Security.
auto-video: Generating videos via ComfyUI/Wan2.2, describing video concepts, scene composition.
auto-vision: Image understanding, visual analysis, multimodal tasks with attached images, diagram interpretation, object recognition, screenshot analysis.
tools-specialist: Direct tool invocation and MCP server operations: file system access, git operations, code execution, fetch, Docker management — use when you need raw tool access, not an AI workspace.
